name: Beef in Pepper Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Boneless beef sirloin, trimmed of fat and cut into inch strips
2 Red bell peppers, one chopped and one cut into 1/2-inch strips
1 Jalapeno pepper, seeded and cut into 1/4-inch strips
Hot cooked rice or pita bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a blender on medium-high speed, blend the red pepper, jalapeno, garlic, white wine, ginger, salt, pepper, turmeric, cardamom, and hot sauce until smoothly blended.
2. Heat the butter or margarine and oil in a 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat until hot.
3. Add the beef and cook until most of the liquid has been rendered and the meat is browned on all sides, about 3 to 4 minutes. Remove the beef and keep warm.
4. Add the onion and red pepper strips to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 3 minutes.
5. Return the beef to the skillet and add the blended mixture. Heat to boiling, then reduce the heat and simmer uncovered until the beef is hot and the sauce is slightly thickened.
6. Serve over rice or stuffed into pitas, with rice on the side.
